title: Bernie Sanders - Request (Feel the Bern!!!=I like Bernie Sanders)

enrichment.summary: Kyle endorses Bernie Sanders for president, arguing he has a genuine record of caring for ordinary people unlike Hillary Clinton or Republicans. He discusses whether Congress would block Sanders' agenda (yes, at first, but predicts eventual populist backlash), whether Sanders could be assassinated for challenging the status quo (unlikely unless he opposes military interventions), and compares him favorably to Obama while critiquing Hillary's use of gender identity politics.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Should be next president; has a genuine record and doesn't take donor money
Disqualified by taking Wall Street speaking fees; using gender as a substitute for a good record
A Jewish president would be more historically significant given anti-Semitism's deeper history than anti-womanism
Congress will block him initially but will get voted out as public opinion shifts
Works fine — European socialist countries are better off than the US; Fox News critics are uneducated on the topic
Only real risk would be if he refused military deployments; unlikely because power elite fears getting caught
Too timid and 'Uncle Tom'; still blocked by Republicans despite being politically moderate
A fascist who manipulates people and exploits eminent domain to benefit elites
Idea is somewhat sloppy but fundable if military spending is redirected

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders

claims.subject: Hillary Clinton

claims.subject: Jeb Bush

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders

claims.claim: Bernie Sanders has a longer and more solid track record than Barack Obama.

claims.subject: Barack Obama
claims.claim: Barack Obama was elected partly because he was black.

claims.subject: Hillary Clinton
claims.claim: Hillary Clinton is using her gender as a reason to get elected.

claims.subject: Madeline Albright
claims.claim: Madeline Albright said there's a special place in hell for women who don't vote for Hillary.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Having a Jewish president like Bernie Sanders would be more revolutionary than having a female president.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: There's a lot more anti-Semitism in the world than anti-womanism.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Getting a Jewish president would require social change and acceptance.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders could face more drama from Republicans than a woman president would.

claims.subject: Barack Obama
claims.claim: Republicans have blocked everything Barack Obama tried to do because he's black.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders would be the oldest person ever elected president.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Congress would try to block Bernie Sanders but could be voted out if they do.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders is challenging the status quo of the rich and powerful.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders is expressing what the majority of people want, like taxing the wealthy.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: The only way the power elite would assassinate Bernie Sanders is if he refused to go to war.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders would appoint very liberal people to his administration.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders can't be framed or impeached because he's been in public office for so long without scandal.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders will be the first of many liberal leaders as the status quo crumbles.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders will apply more taxes to the rich and reduce warfare.

claims.subject: Bernie Sanders
claims.claim: Bernie Sanders will fund good education and other programs by reallocating money from wasteful spending.

transcript.text: Okay, taking a quick break to do a request video. I'm ever so, uh, I can't remember what I was going to say. Okay, so let's, oh yeah, I'm ever so happy that people are giving me request videos.

So here's one. This is about Bernie Sanders. Request video. What do you think of Bernie Sanders? And should he be our next president? Also, if he was elected, could he actually get any of what he wants done? Or would Congress block everything he tries to do? From a Charlie Spencer. And then Will Burstyn goes. I would add to Spencer's question this one. Would Bernie be assassinated if he challenges the status quo?

The way he says he will, I guess it would be easier for them to frame and impeach him, but it's an interesting thing to think about it. Okay. Um, do I think Bernie Sanders should be? Yes, I think Bernie Sanders should be our next president. He's the perfect guy. We're there. We hit it. We got a good guy this time. He's a guy who actually, uh, you know, um, cares about people. He doesn't take the donor money. Um, you know, like the other guys do. Um, and he says the right things about how he's going to take out the bankers. Now the question is, you know, is he like saying the truth? Like, is he really care? You know? Um, yeah, I think, I think, you know, you can believe that he really cares because of his record and because of what he's saying.
Um, and, uh, and, uh, oh my God, they're like a whole world's different from the, um, Republicans.

And it's like, no, if you are like, people are supposed to be in your car, then you should make enough to have a good life.
And you just like even the poorest person. And so that's, that's the framework that he's coming from, the angle that he's coming from.
